per_prediction_verdicts.reasoning: S-wave shadow zone is Oldham (1906)/Gutenberg (1914) textbook seismology. The dome's 120-160 degree range is narrower than the actual ~104-180 degree shadow — so the dome is not only claiming standard physics but getting the range wrong. Additionally, the shadow zone REQUIRES a spherical layered Earth with a liquid outer core, which the dome model denies. This is self-defeating evidence: the dome claims a phenomenon as confirmation that can only exist if the dome is wrong. Previous batch review noted this pattern for PRED-084 (P-waves); it applies equally here for S-waves.

Indian Ocean Geoid Low known since 1970s, mapped by GRACE/GOCE/EGM2008. Predicting stationarity of a feature documented for 50+ years is definitionally retrodiction. Standard mantle convection models explain stability. Assessment is solid.

Verdict is correct. CAVEAT: The assessment claims 70N/135W is within the 'broad influence zone of the Hudson Bay gravity low.' This is geographically imprecise — Hudson Bay anomaly center is at ~60N/85W, about 2,500 km away. The coordinates 70N/135W are in the Beaufort Sea near the Mackenzie Delta, influenced by Beaufort-Mackenzie sedimentary basin and post-glacial rebound from the Laurentide ice sheet. There IS a real gravity anomaly here, but attributing it to Hudson Bay is a stretch a geophysicist could poke at. Also, testability_score 'medium' is inconsistent with PRED-087 and PRED-111 (both 'low') when all three are satellite-mapped features checkable in published gravity models. Should be 'low'.

This prediction substantively restates WIN-066 (NH heat excess asymmetry, Std Model Explains). The assessment explicitly identifies the overlap: 'WIN-066 already addresses the NH heat excess claim.' Yet the verdict is 'standard_physics' while PRED-094 (overlaps WIN-001), PRED-099 (overlaps WIN-065), and PRED-100 (overlaps WIN-056) are all verdicted 'recycled.' The distinction appears to be whether restates_win is populated in predictions.json, not whether the prediction substantively restates a WIN. This is an exploitable inconsistency — a dome defender can argue we apply 'recycled' selectively. RECOMMENDATION: Change to 'recycled' with restates_win='066', consistent with how other WIN-restating predictions are handled.

Clear recycle of WIN-001. Tesla 11.79 Hz is the same 11.78 Hz claim with an extra decimal. Assessment correctly identifies no spectral peak exists between first and second Schumann modes. DATA FIX NEEDED: restates_win is null in predictions.json despite the assessment naming WIN-001 as the source. Set restates_win='001'.

Restates_win correctly fixed from 055 to 048 (CMB Axis of Evil). The verdict 'recycled' is correct. DATA HYGIENE ISSUE: The notes field in predictions.json still says 'WIN-055 is verdicted Misleading: the CMB anomalies are within standard cosmic variance.' WIN-055 is Distance-redshift Cepheid/SBF, NOT CMB. This should reference WIN-048. Also, the assessment file (PRED-assessment-PRED-097.json) still says 'Restates WIN-055.' Both need updating for consistency.

Clear recycle of WIN-065 (Polaris systematic excess). The +0.36 degree figure is post-hoc fitted to the known Polaris-pole offset. The prediction's own notes admit it contradicts W024 (falsified), meaning the dome's Polaris predictions are internally inconsistent. DATA FIX: restates_win should be '065' (currently null).

The formula H(phi)=90-|phi+theta| IS the globe's standard astronomical solar noon elevation formula. WIN-056 documents this as self-contradicted (covertly uses globe declination). Assessment's testability_score of 'none' is appropriate — this isn't a test, it's an identity. DATA FIX: restates_win should be '056' (currently null).
Known GRACE/GOCE anomaly from Southwest Indian Ridge at 30S/60E. Publicly mapped since mid-2000s. Assessment is thorough. No challenge.

Mascon positional stability on decadal timescales is a default expectation. Assessment correctly notes amplitude changes (ice loss, groundwater depletion) even when positions are stable. The 2028+ window makes this nearly unfalsifiable in practice, but standard_physics is the right verdict because the claim is trivially correct under all cosmological models.
Tsumeb gravity anomaly from Otavi carbonate platform and Congo craton edge. Known from GRACE since 2002. Assessment correctly links to WIN-011-014 pattern. No challenge.

Atmospheric refraction lifting apparent lunar position quantified since Tycho Brahe (1590s). Assessment makes an excellent additional point: dome geometry (moon at 2,534 km) would produce DIFFERENT refraction behavior than observed. This is a discriminating test that goes AGAINST the dome. Strong assessment.

The assessment says 'even the kernel of truth — that Earth does transmit mechanical waves at comparable speeds — is standard seismology relabeled.' The 9.2 km/s value sits within known P-wave velocities (8-13 km/s in upper mantle). This isn't unfalsifiable — it's relabeled standard seismology. The test is trivially passed by standard geophysics. 'standard_physics' is the more accurate verdict: we tested it and it matches what we already knew. 'Unfalsifiable' gives the dome an unearned grievance ('you won't even test it!') when the reality is worse: it HAS been tested and matches standard physics, not dome predictions. This is the same relabels_standard pattern documented in Part 2b. RECOMMENDATION: Change to 'standard_physics' with note about probable reverse-engineering from published seismic velocity profiles.

No measurement protocol exists for 'aetheric medium density.' The value (3.4e17 kg/m3, neutron-star territory) has no observational analogue in Earth science. Unlike PRED-NEW-002 where the value matches known data, this density value corresponds to nothing measurable on Earth. Genuinely unfalsifiable. A dome defender could argue it's derived from dome equations (v=sqrt(B/rho)), but circular derivations are unfalsifiable by design, which is exactly the problem.

Catastrophically falsified by 5-7 orders of magnitude. Author agrees. Gaia DR3 1.8 billion stars at microarcsecond precision vs. predicted 3 degree scatter. Exemplar falsification — should be highlighted in predictions summary. Bulletproof.

The prediction claimed poleward drift dominates over equatorward. Author's own REFINED status concedes lateral drift dominates, not poleward. The prediction's core directional claim is directly contradicted by observations. This should be 'falsified' not 'standard_physics.' The standard_physics verdict implies the underlying phenomenon exists but is already explained — true but understates the failure. The dome predicted poleward dominance; reality shows lateral dominance. That IS falsification. The assessment even notes the REFINED status but doesn't follow through to the logical conclusion. RECOMMENDATION: Change to 'falsified.'

The prediction specified '>=3% anomaly.' Author's REFINED status concedes smaller effect than predicted. If the dome set a quantitative threshold and the observed effect fell short, the specific claim failed. This is falsification, not unfalsifiability — 'unfalsifiable' means no observation can distinguish success from failure, but here the dome defined its own success criterion (>=3%) and the observation fell short. A dome defender cannot simultaneously claim >=3% and say the prediction is untestable. RECOMMENDATION: Change to 'falsified' with note that if the measurement protocol is too vague to pin down, document that ambiguity as an escape clause but still note the author's concession. The Roaring 40s wind band is fully explained by Ferrel cell circulation on a globe — the SAA correlation is spurious.
Author self-classified as falsified. No elevation excess observed above 55N. WIN-065 documents refutation. Clean.

Predicted enhancement during G3+ storms; opposite (suppression) observed. Author agrees. Standard ionospheric physics (Schlegel and Fullekrug 1999) predicts the observed suppression. Note: PRED-SR-SUPPRESS reverses this claim, predicting suppression instead — the dome moved to the opposite pole after falsification. This reversal pattern should be highlighted.

Perspective projection of parallel light through atmosphere — textbook optics. Anticrepuscular rays disprove local sun geometry (impossible with a nearby light source). WIN-026 covers this. No challenge.

Non-discriminating. Any model reproducing obliquity of the ecliptic reproduces equinox day lengths. Dome provides no quantitative formula that differs from standard astronomy. Author's own 'unresolved' status is appropriate. No challenge.

challenges.reason: Substantively restates WIN-066 but not classified as recycled. Taxonomy inconsistency with PRED-094/099/100.
Prediction's directional claim (poleward > equatorward) contradicted by observations; author's REFINED status concedes failure.
Quantitative threshold (>=3%) not met; author's REFINED status concedes smaller effect. Testable claim that failed, not unfalsifiable.
9.2 km/s matches known P-wave velocities (8-13 km/s). Relabeled seismology, not untestable claim.

challenges.recommended_verdict: no verdict change — data fix only
challenges.reason: restates_win fields are null despite assessments naming specific source WINs. Fix: 094→001, 099→065, 100→056.

data_fixes_needed: PRED-097: Fix notes field to reference WIN-048 instead of WIN-055
PRED-088: Fix geographic attribution (Beaufort-Mackenzie, not Hudson Bay), downgrade testability_score to 'low'

cross_cutting_observations.detail: Five predictions (PRED-087, 088, 111, 112, GRAV-001) mine publicly available GRACE/GOCE/EGM satellite gravimetry data. All reference features mapped years before registration. This is systematic retrodiction from published geophysical datasets — the geodetic equivalent of the Schumann resonance pattern. Consider compiling geodetic prediction statistics for Part 6 or Part 2b.

W019 and W020 have author_status 'refined' — meaning the author's prediction failed its original criterion and was retroactively weakened. Our verdicts (standard_physics, unfalsifiable) don't capture this failure. When the dome author concedes refinement because reality disagreed, that IS falsification of the original prediction. Currently, refined predictions disappear into less critical categories, understating the dome model's actual failure rate.

PRED-085 (S-wave shadow) requires spherical layered Earth with liquid outer core — the exact structure the dome denies. This matches PRED-084 (P-wave shadow) from the previous batch. The dome claims phenomena as confirmations that can only exist if the dome is wrong. Pattern worth highlighting in Part 2b.

Three entries touch Polaris: W024 (falsified, no excess at >55N), PRED-099 (recycled, claims +0.36 at 45N), PRED-PARALLAX-001 (falsified, scatter >3 degrees off by 5+ orders of magnitude). The dome's Polaris predictions contradict each other across the catalog.

cross_cutting_observations.observation: Author-acknowledged falsifications are powerful but underused
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Three predictions (W024, W027-PRED-R006, PRED-PARALLAX-001) are self-classified as falsified by the dome author. Two more (W019, W020) have REFINED status acknowledging failure. These author concessions preempt the 'biased reviewer' counterargument and deserve aggregate treatment in the predictions summary. Currently they appear only in individual entries.

overall_assessment: The Wave 3b assessments are competent but systematically too lenient in two patterns. First, falsification undercounting: predictions W019 and W020, whose authors conceded failure via REFINED status, are classified as standard_physics and unfalsifiable rather than falsified. When the dome's own author admits a prediction failed, calling it anything else understates the model's failure rate. Second, three recycled predictions have null restates_win fields, and PRED-091 (which substantively restates WIN-066) isn't classified as recycled at all. This creates exploitable taxonomy inconsistencies. The PRED-NEW-002 challenge is about framing: 'unfalsifiable' sounds better for the dome than 'matches standard physics,' and we should consistently choose the most accurate framing. Overall: 5 verdicts challenged (3 too lenient, 1 verdict mismatch, 1 taxonomy inconsistency), 3 agreed with caveats, 13 straight agreed. No verdicts were found too harsh — we are not being unfair to the dome model.
